title: Integrated immunoassay
abstract: Microfluidic devices and systems are provided. Methods for conducting immune assays with the devices and systems are also provided.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of determining an immunological phenotype of a plurality of white blood cells in a sample comprising: (a) introducing a sample comprising a plurality of white blood cells into a sample reservoir of a microfluidic device; (b) dispensing, by electromechanical droplet manipulation, a droplet of said sample comprising a cell from said sample reservoir into a channel operably connected to said sample reservoir; (c) moving said droplet, by electromechanical droplet manipulation, along said channel to a first immobilization electrode; (d) determining whether or not said cell is immobilized on said first immobilization electrode; (e) determining a subtype of said cell based on step (d); (f) moving said droplet comprising said cell, by electromechanical droplet manipulation, from said first immobilization electrode along a channel in a direction based on said subtype of said cell determined in step (d); (g) determining a final phenotypic subtype of said cell; and (h) moving said droplet comprising said cell, by electromechanical droplet manipulation, along a channel to a cell collection reservoir based on such final phenotypic subtype of said cell determined in step (g). 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: (i) repeating steps (b)-(h) for a second droplet of said sample, wherein said second droplet comprises a cell. 3. The method of any one of claims 1-2, wherein step (g) comprises determining whether or not said cell is immobilized on one or more immobilization electrodes. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein said one or more immobilization electrodes are selected from an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD4 

 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-Î±Î² antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-LgM antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA antibody, and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA-DR antibody. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein step (g) comprises determining a cell subtype selected from CD4 positive alpha beta cells, CD4 positive gamma delta cells, CD8 positive alpha beta cells, CD8 positive gamma delta cells, NK cells, mature B cells, naive B cells, HLA positive cells, HLA-DR positive cells, and dendritic cells. 6. The method of claim 2, comprising concurrently identifying two or more cell subtypes selected from CD4 positive alpha beta cells, CD4 positive gamma delta cells, CD8 positive alpha beta cells, CD8 positive gamma delta cells, NK cells, mature B cells, naive B cells, HLA positive cells, HLA-DR positive cells, and dendritic cells. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, further comprising: (j) independently collecting cells of one or more subtypes to create one or more pools of cells having the same subtype. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, further comprising: (k) lysing one or more pools of cells having the same subtype to release cellular contents. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising: (1) analyzing said cellular contents. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein analyzing said cellular contents comprises separating said cellular contents into an RNA component and a cellular protein component. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein analyzing said cellular contents comprises analyzing a component selected from said RNA component and said cellular protein component 12. The method of claim 11, wherein analyzing said cellular contents comprises analyzing said cellular protein component. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein analyzing said cellular contents comprises passing said cellular protein component through a filter. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein said filter is a synthetic nuclear membrane. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein said filter is an artificial cell, a liposome, a polymersome, or a microfilter. 16. The method of claim 11, wherein analyzing said cellular contents comprises analyzing said RNA component. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein analyzing said RNA component comprises conducting genome wide RNA sequencing. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein analyzing said RNA component comprises PCR microarray analysis of target genes. 19. A microfluidic device comprising: a first substrate; a second substrate; and 

 a fluid layer disposed between said first substrate and said second substrate and comprising: a first sample reservoir; a first reagent reservoir; a first cell collection reservoir; a second cell collection reservoir; a channel network connecting said first and second reservoirs; and an electrode layer disposed between said first substrate and said fluid layer, wherein said electrode layer comprises a first immobilization electrode aligned within a first portion of said channel network of said fluid layer, wherein said first immobilization electrode is configured to immobilize a cell having a first target surface marker, wherein said first portion of said channel network is positioned between said sample reservoir and each of said first cell collection reservoir and said second cell collection reservoir. 20. The microfluidic device of claim 19, wherein said first immobilization electrode comprises immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 21. The microfluidic device of claim 20, further comprising one or more electrodes selected from an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD4 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-Î±Î² antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-LgM antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA antibody, and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA- DR antibody. 22. A microfluidic device configured to concurrently identify, from a single sample of white blood cells, two or more cell subtypes selected from CD4 positive alpha beta cells, CD4 positive gamma delta cells, CD8 positive alpha beta cells, CD8 positive gamma delta cells, K cells, mature B cells, naive B cells, HLA positive cells, HLA-DR positive cells, and dendritic cells. 23. The microfluidic device of claim 22, further configured to quantify the number of cells in each of said two or more cell subtypes. 24. The microfluidic device of any one of claims 22-23, wherein said device comprises three or more electrodes selected from an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD4 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-Î±Î² antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-LgM antibody, an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA antibody, and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA-DR antibody. 25. The microfluidic device of claim 24, wherein said device is configured to identify said two or more cell subtypes by contacting one or more droplets of said sample with two or more of said electrodes. 26. The microfluidic device of claim 24, comprising a channel network comprising said one or more electrodes, wherein said channel network comprises one or more channels in which sample flows from an upstream portion at a sample reservoir to a downstream portion away from said sample reservoir in a downstream direction. 27. The microfluidic device of claim 26 comprising: 

 an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD4 antibody; and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-Î±Î² antibody, wherein said electrode comprising immobilized anti-Î±Î² antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD4 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD4 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, and said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 28. The microfluidic device of claim 27 comprising: an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody; and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-Î±Î² antibody, wherein said electrode comprising immobilized anti-Î±Î² antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD8 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, and said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 29. The microfluidic device of claim 26 comprising: an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody; and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, wherein said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, and said 

 electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 30. The microfluidic device of claim 26 comprising: an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody; and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody, wherein said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, and said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 31. The microfluidic device of claim 26 comprising: an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody; and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-LgM antibody, wherein said electrode comprising immobilized anti-LgM antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, and said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 32. The microfluidic device of claim 26 comprising: an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody; and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA antibody, wherein said electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti- CD123 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, and said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 33. The microfluidic device of claim 26 comprising: an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody; an electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody; and an electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA-DR antibody, wherein said electrode comprising immobilized anti-HLA-DR antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD14 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD20 antibody, said electrode comprising 

 immobilized anti-CD20 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD123 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti- CD123 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody, said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD7 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody, and said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD3 antibody is downstream of said electrode comprising immobilized anti-CD45 antibody. 34. A system comprising: a microfluidic device selected from the microfluidic device of any one of claims 19-21 and the microfluidic device of any one of claims 22-33; a control circuit; a power supply; and a graphical user interface. 35. A method of analyzing, independently, the RNA of a single cell subtype in a sample comprising a plurality of cell subtypes comprising: (a) splitting said sample into one or more cell subtypes by passing at least a portion of said sample through a microfluidic device to create one or more pools of cells having the same subtype; (b) lysing one or more pools of cells having the same subtype to release cellular contents; (c) separating said cellular contents into an RNA component and a cellular protein component; and (d) analyzing said RNA component. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein said microfluidic device is selected from the microfluidic device of any one of claims 19-21 and the microfluidic device of any one of claims 22-33. 37. A method of analyzing signal transduction proteins of a single cell subtype in a sample comprising a plurality of cell subtypes comprising: 

 (a) splitting said sample into one or more cell subtypes by passing at least a portion of said sample through a microfluidic device to create one or more pools of cells having the same subtype; (b) lysing one or more pools of cells having the same subtype to release cellular contents; (c) separating said cellular contents into an RNA component and a cellular protein component; and (d) analyzing said cellular protein component. 38. The method of claim 37, wherein said microfluidic device is selected from the microfluidic device of any one of claims 19-21 and the microfluidic device of any one of claims 22-33. 39. A method of determining a disease phenotype of an immune mediated disease in a patient having said immune mediated disease comprising: (a) splitting said sample into one or more cell subtypes by passing at least a portion of said sample through a microfluidic device to create one or more pools of cells having the same subtype; (b) lysing one or more pools of cells having the same subtype to release cellular contents; (c) separating said cellular contents into an RNA component and a cellular protein component; (d) analyzing at least one of said RNA component and said cellular protein component; and (e) determining said disease phenotype based on step (d). 40. The method of claim 39, wherein said immune mediated disease is selected from Crohn's disease, multiple sclerosis, rheumatoid arthritis, and inflammatory bowel disease. 41. The method of claim 40, wherein said immune mediated disease is Crohn's disease and wherein said disease phenotype is selected from severe Crohn's disease and indolent Crohn's disease. 42. The method of claim 41, wherein said microfluidic device is selected from the microfluidic device of any one of claims 19-21 and the microfluidic device of any one of claims 22-33. 43. A method of determining an immunological phenotype of a plurality of white blood cells in a sample comprising: (a) identifying a cell subtype for each of two or more of said white blood cells; (b) quantifying each cell subtype; (c) isolating and pooling cells of each subtype into one or more subtype pools; (d) lysing cells of each of said one or more subtype pools to yield one or more cellular content pools; (e) separating said cellular content pools of each cell subtype into an RNA component and a protein component; and (f) analyzing at least one of said RNA component and said protein component. 44. The method of claim 43, wherein step (a) comprises moving one or more droplets of said sample through a channel network of a microfluidic device. 45. The method of any one of claims 43-44, wherein step (a) comprises moving one or more droplets of said sample through a microfluidic device selected from the microfluidic device of any one of claims 19-21 and the microfluidic device of any one of claims 22-33. A microfluidic device comprising: at least one sample reservoir; 

 a first channel operably connect to said sample reservoir; a first electrode disposed within said first channel and downstream of said sample reservoir; a second channel; a third channel; a first channel junction disposed downstream of said first electrode and connecting said first channel to at least said second channel and said third channel; a second electrode disposed downstream of said first electrode and at or upstream of said first channel junction; a fourth channel; a fifth channel; a second channel junction disposed downstream of said first channel junction and connecting said second channel to at least said fourth channel and said fifth channel; a third electrode disposed downstream of said first channel junction and at, upstream of, or downstream and adjacent to said second channel junction, wherein when said third electrode is disposed downstream and adjacent to said second channel junction, said third electrode is disposed within said fifth channel; a fourth electrode disposed downstream of said first channel junction and at, upstream of, or downstream and adjacent to said second channel junction, wherein when said fourth electrode is disposed downstream and adjacent to said second channel junction, said fourth electrode is disposed within said fourth channel; a sixth channel; a seventh channel; a third channel junction disposed downstream of said second channel junction and connecting said fourth channel to at least said sixth channel and said seventh channel; a fifth electrode disposed at or upstream of said third channel junction and downstream of said second channel junction; and 

 a first cell collection reservoir connected to said sixth channel and disposed downstream of said third channel junction. The microfluidic device of claim 46, further comprising: a second cell collection reservoir connected to said seventh channel and disposed downstream of said third channel junction. The microfluidic device of claim 47, further comprising: a fourth channel junction disposed downstream of said second channel junction and connecting said fifth channel to at least said eighth channel and said ninth channel; a sixth electrode disposed at or upstream of said fourth channel junction and downstream of said second channel junction; and a third cell collection reservoir connected to said eighth channel and disposed downstream of said fourth channel junction. 49. The microfluidic device of claim 48, further comprising: a fourth cell collection reservoir connected to said seventh channel and disposed downstream of said fourth channel junction. The microfluidic device of claim 49, further comprising: a tenth channel; an eleventh channel; a fifth channel junction disposed downstream of said second electrode and connecting said third channel to at least said tenth channel and said eleventh channel; a seventh electrode disposed downstream of said first channel junction and at or upstream of said fifth channel junction; and a fifth cell collection reservoir connected to said tenth channel and disposed downstream of said fifth channel junction. The microfluidic device of claim 50, further compri 

 a twelfth channel; a thirteenth channel; a sixth channel junction disposed downstream of said seventh electrode and connecting said eleventh channel to at least said twelfth channel and said thirteenth channel; an eighth electrode disposed downstream of said fifth channel junction and at or upstream of said sixth channel junction; and a sixth cell collection reservoir connected to said thirteenth channel and disposed downstream of said sixth channel junction. The microfluidic device of claim 51, further comprising: a fourteenth channel; a fifteenth channel; a seventh channel junction disposed downstream of said sixth channel junction and connecting said twelfth channel to at least said fourteenth channel and said fifteenth channel; a ninth electrode disposed downstream of said sixth channel junction and at, upstream of, or downstream and adjacent to said seventh channel junction, wherein when said ninth electrode is disposed downstream and adjacent to said seventh channel junction, said ninth electrode is disposed within said fifteenth channel; a tenth electrode disposed downstream of said sixth channel junction and at, upstream of, or downstream and adjacent to said seventh channel junction, wherein when said tenth electrode is disposed downstream and adjacent to said sixth channel junction, said tenth electrode is disposed within said fifteenth channel; a sixteenth channel; a seventeenth channel; an eighth channel junction disposed downstream of said seventh channel junction and connecting said fourteenth channel to at least said sixteenth channel and said seventeenth channel; 

 an eleventh electrode disposed downstream of said seventh channel junction and at or upstream of said eighth channel junction; and a seventh cell collection reservoir connected to said sixteenth channel and disposed downstream of said eighth channel junction. 53. The microfluidic device of claim 52, further comprising: an eighth cell collection reservoir connected to said seventeenth channel and disposed downstream of said eighth channel junction. 54. The microfluidic device of claim 53, further comprising: an eighteenth channel; a nineteenth channel; a ninth channel junction disposed downstream of said seventh channel junction and connecting said fifteenth channel to at least said eighteenth channel and said nineteenth channel; a twelfth electrode disposed downstream of said seventh channel junction and at, upstream of, or downstream and adjacent to said ninth channel junction, wherein when said twelfth electrode is disposed downstream and adjacent to said ninth channel junction, said twelfth electrode is disposed within said eighteenth channel; a thirteenth electrode disposed downstream of said seventh channel junction and at, upstream of, or downstream and adjacent to said ninth channel junction, wherein when said eleventh electrode is disposed downstream and adjacent to said ninth channel junction, said eleventh electrode is disposed within said nineteenth channel; and a ninth cell collection reservoir connected to said eighteenth channel and disposed downstream of said ninth channel junction. 55. The microfluidic device of claim 54, further comprising: a tenth cell collection reservoir connected to said nineteenth channel and disposed downstream of said ninth channel junction. 56. The microfluidic device of claim 55, further comprising: at least one alternate reservoir operatively linked to at least a portion of any one of said first through nineteenth channels by a twentieth channel. 57. The microfluidic device of claim 56, wherein said at least one alternate reservoir is selected from disposal reservoir, a first reagent reservoir, a second reagent reservoir, and combinations thereof. 58. The microfluidic device of claim 56, wherein said at least one alternate reservoir is a combination of a disposal reservoir, a first reagent reservoir, and a second reagent reservoir, and said first reagent reservoir and said second reagent reservoir are operatively linked via one or more channels. 59. The microfluidic device of claim 56, wherein said at least one alternate reservoir is a combination of a first reagent reservoir, and a second reagent reservoir, and wherein said device further comprises a mixing zone for mixing a first reagent and a second reagent on said device. 60. The microfluidic device of claim 56, wherein said at least one alternate reservoir is operatively linked to said first channel junction by said twentieth channel. 61. The microfluidic device of claim 55, wherein: said first electrode comprises anti-CD45 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said first electrode; said second electrode comprises anti-CD3 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said second electrode; said third electrode comprises anti-CD8 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said third electrode; said fourth electrode comprises anti-CD4 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said fourth electrode; said fifth electrode comprises anti-Î±Î² antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said fifth electrode; 

 said sixth electrode comprises anti- Î±Î² antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said sixth electrode; said seventh comprises anti-CD7 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said seventh electrode; said eighth comprises anti-CD123 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said eighth electrode; said ninth comprises anti-CD20 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said ninth electrode; said tenth comprises anti-CD14 antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said tenth electrode; said eleventh comprises anti-IgM antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said eleventh electrode; said twelfth comprises anti-HLA antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said twelfth electrode; said thirteenth comprises anti-HLA-DR antibody immobilized on a channel surface of said thirteenth electrode; and wherein said channel surface of each electrode is independently disposed within a channel of said device and configured to contact a droplet of a sample passing through said channels of said device. 62. A system comprising: the microfluidic device of any one of claims 46-61; a control circuit; a power supply; and a graphical user interface. 63. A method of analyzing, independently, the RNA of a single cell subtype in a sample comprising a plurality of cell subtypes comprising: (a) splitting said sample into one or more cell subtypes by passing at least a portion of said sample through a microfluidic device to create one or more pools of cells having the same subtype; 

 (b) lysing one or more pools of cells having the same subtype to release cellular contents; (c) separating said cellular contents into an RNA component and a cellular protein component; and (d) analyzing said RNA component, wherein said microfluidic device is the microfluidic device of any one of claims 46- 61. 64. A method of analyzing signal transduction proteins of a single cell subtype in a sample comprising a plurality of cell subtypes comprising: (a) splitting said sample into one or more cell subtypes by passing at least a portion of said sample through a microfluidic device to create one or more pools of cells having the same subtype; (b) lysing one or more pools of cells having the same subtype to release cellular contents; (c) separating said cellular contents into an RNA component and a cellular protein component; and (d) analyzing said cellular protein component, wherein said microfluidic device is the microfluidic device of any one of claims 46- 61. 65. A method of determining a disease phenotype of an immune mediated disease in a patient having said immune mediated disease comprising: (a) splitting said sample into one or more cell subtypes by passing at least a portion of said sample through a microfluidic device to create one or more pools of cells having the same subtype; (b) lysing one or more pools of cells having the same subtype to release cellular contents; (c) separating said cellular contents into an RNA component and a cellular protein component; (d) analyzing at least one of said RNA component and said cellular protein component; and 

 (e) determining said disease phenotype based on step (d), wherein said microfluidic device is the microfluidic device of any one of claims 46- 61. 66. The method of claim 65, wherein said immune mediated disease is selected from Crohn's disease, multiple sclerosis, rheumatoid arthritis, and inflammatory bowel disease. 67. The method of claim 66, wherein said immune mediated disease is Crohn's disease and wherein said disease phenotype is selected from severe Crohn's disease and indolent Crohn's disease. 68. A method of determining an immunological phenotype of a plurality of white blood cells in a sample comprising: (a) identifying a cell subtype for each of two or more of said white blood cells; (b) quantifying each cell subtype; (c) isolating and pooling cells of each subtype into one or more subtype pools; (d) lysing cells of each of said one or more subtype pools to yield one or more cellular content pools; (e) separating said cellular content pools of each cell subtype into an RNA component and a protein component; and (f) analyzing at least one of said RNA component and said protein component, wherein step (a) comprises moving one or more droplets of said sample through the microfluidic device of any one of claims 46-61.
